episodes.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick faces heat over 2012 Epstein island visit
episodes.description:
'엡스타인 스캔들' 러트닉 美 상무장관 위증 논란 의회 청문회 곤욕

In the U.S., Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told congressional investigators he cannot explain why he and his family had lunch on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein’s private island in 2012. 
His answers on Wednesday contradict his past public statements about cutting ties with Epstein years earlier. Lutnick had previously claimed he distanced himself from Epstein around 2005 after an uncomfortable encounter. Justice Department files released in January showed the 2012 island visit and emails showing Lutnick later invited Epstein to a 2015 fundraiser for Hillary Clinton.

episodes.title: Bank of America to pay US$ 72.5 million to settle Epstein accusers' lawsuit
episodes.description:
뱅크오브아메리카, 엡스틴 피해자 소송에 7,250만 달러 합의금 지급

A Manhattan federal court on Friday accepted Bank of America’s agreement to pay 72-point-5 million U.S. dollars to women who accused the bank of enabling Jeffrey Epstein’s sexual abuse.
Plaintiffs alleged the bank ignored suspicious transactions tied to Epstein, choosing profit over victim protection. Bank of America denied wrongdoing but called the settlement the right path forward.
Other banks made similar payments in 2023, with JPMorgan settling for 290 million dollars, and Deutsche Bank for 75 million.
A hearing to consider final approval is scheduled for Thursday.
